ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George McNitt for and with Charles Harris. Christine James for and with Paul Muscat. James Muscat also present.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Harris being removed/suspended as Trustee, James Muscat consenting to serve as Successor Trustee, delays by both Parties, Mr. Harris not having a due process hearing/evidentiary hearing, Mr. Harris not providing additional documents to the Court, mediation, surcharges, the accountings and setting the matter for trial. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court suspended Charles Harris' powers as Trustee pending trial and referred the matter to the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) for trial setting on the accountings. Ms. James is to get the consent to serve as Successor Trustee from James Muscat and file it, and to prepare the order suspending Charles Harris' powers and appointing James Muscat as Temporary Successor Trustee.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-10-2010 Continued To Jun-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Welty for Charles Harris (Trustee).

Mr. Welty stated that Ms. James was not appearing today, that she tried to request a continuance and that he was here to request that the matter be continued. In addition, he stated that he was going to withdraw from the case tomorrow and that Mr. Harris will have new Counsel substitute in. The matter was continued to June 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar May-10-2010 Continued To Jun-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Continued To May-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Welty for and with Charles Harris (Trustee). Christine James appeared by telephone for and with Paul Muscat (present in Court).

The Court informed Ms. James that a proper petition (amended petition/amendment to the petition) namimg the Successor Trustee with proper notice was needed. The Court also informed Mr. Welty that a complete accounting from July 29, 1992 through December 31, 2009 was also needed. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accountings, payment to Mr. Harris' Son for managing the Trust accounts and loss of funds. The matter was continued to May 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the full accounting and the Petition to Appoint a Successor Trustee. Original bank statements are required. The issue regarding removal of Mr. Harris as Trustee will not be addressed until the Court has the opportunity to review the entire/full accountings.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Jan-25-2010 Continued To Mar-15-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court continued this matter to March 15, 2010, the same date that the "Amended Annual Trust Accountings 2004-2008 and Declaration re: ATM withdrawals" is set. The Court stated that there are still concerns with both petitions. As to this petition for removal of Trustee (etc.), Counsel still needs to file an amended petition specifying who should serve as Successor Trustee and cure the notice issues. In regard to the "Amended Annual Trust Accountings 2004-2008 and Declaration re: ATM withdrawals" which is currently set for March 15, 2010 the Court notes that there are also service issues as to that matter, that the proof of service is not complete; addresses are missing, and the verification is not dated.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Continued To Jan-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Welty for Charles Harris (Trustee).

Mr. Welty stated that Ms. James was out of town, that she filed her declaration a week ago and that they agreed to continue the matter to the first week in January. The matter was continued to January 25, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. James is to amend the petition designating the Successor Trustee, identifying all of the trust Beneficiaries, their ages and their family relationships. Mr. Welty is to file a declaration regarding the ATM withdrawals and the filing date of the amended account. All of the above documents must be filed by January 19, 2010 close of business day.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Welty for Charles Harris (Trustee). Christine James appeared by telephone for Paul Muscat.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accountings, the proceeds from the sale of the property, the difference between the ending balance in 2002 and the beginning balance in 2003 and the manner in which the funds had been invested. The Court directed Mr. Welty to file a declaration providing information on what happened to funds, information on the ATM withdrawals, and to file an amended accounting with proper notice and a hearing date. The Court also directed Ms. James to file an amended petition for removal addressing who should be the Successor Trustee, identifying all of the trust Beneficiaries, their ages and their family relationships. The Court will not require an accounting for more than 5 years and the accounting petition will be heard before the petition for removal is heard. Status on filing of said petitions was set for December 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. No appearance is required on that date if both petitions have been filed.

Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Welty for Charles Harris (Trustee).

Mr. Welty stated that he spoke to Ms. James last week, that she was under impression that this was a status hearing and that they discussed the accounting. In addition, Mr. Welty stated that 1992 through 1997 was provided to Ms. James which dealt with the house, that the 2008 accounting had been done, that the current accountant could do the accounting, that he contacted a CPA firm that does trust matters and that Ms. James agreed to a continuance after the accounting was done. The Court instructed Mr. Harris to make sure the ATM withdrawals are included in the next report/accounting and to file and serve notice. Furthermore, the Court stated that if Ms. Harris is removed, that a Successor Trustee is needed and that Ms. James' petition did not suggest how the vacancy is to be filled. In addition, the Court stated that Ms. James' petition needed to be amended to indicate how the Successor Trustee is to selected. The matter was continued to November 9, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the accounting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith./J. Shannon.

Due to unavailability of the Court, the matter was continued to November 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Continued To Sep-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Harris (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Christine James appeared by telephone for Paul Muscat (Petitioner).

Both Parties stipulated to Commissioner Sue Kaplan hearing the matter as Judge Pro Tempore. No written objection was received/filed by Mr. Harris. The Court verified that a waiver signed by Teresa Simons had been filed. Ms. James stated that she received an accounting that covered 2003 through 2007, that it was incomplete, that the real property was sold in 1994 for $193,000.00, that there was $100,000.00 already in the account, that she was in the position that the Trustee had not accounted for everything and that the Trustee inappropriately paid his Son. Mr. Harris stated that his Counsel was ill, that he was no longer able to communicate with his Attorney, that he attempted to hire new Counsel, and that family members had access to an atm card and made cash withdrawals. The Court informed Mr. Harris that the accounting is to go back to 2004 to present, is to address the sale of the real property and is to include the atm withdrawals. In addition, the Court stated that it would not suspend Mr. Harris' powers or remove him at this time and that he needed to obtain Counsel to represent him. The matter was continued to September 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of the accounting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Verified Petition For (1) Removal Of Trustee;(2) Accounting;(3)Surcharge;And(4) Approval Of Attorney Fees.
